```asp
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI – deterministic stretch‑towards/away solution
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% ----- domain --------------------------------------------------
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

col(C) :- input(R,C,_).

% ----- wall row (a row that contains a 5 in every column) -----
exists_counterexample(W) :- col(C), not input(W,C,5).
wall_row(W)   :- not exists_counterexample(W).

% ----- colour parity (colours 0..9 are supplied externally) -----
even(0).  even(2).  even(4).  even(6).  even(8).
odd(1).   odd(3).   odd(7).   odd(9).

% ----- keep original non‑zero, non‑5 cells --------------------
output(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R,C,Col),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 5.

% ----- keep wall cells ---------------------------------------
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

% ----- direction for each original cell ----------------------
% Dir = 1 means downwards, Dir = -1 means upwards
direction(R0,C,Col,Dir) :-
    wall_row(W),
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    even(Col), R0 #< W, Dir = 1.
direction(R0,C,Col,Dir) :-
    wall_row(W),
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    even(Col), R0 #> W, Dir = -1.
direction(R0,C,Col,Dir) :-
    wall_row(W),
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    odd(Col),  R0 #< W, Dir = -1.
direction(R0,C,Col,Dir) :-
    wall_row(W),
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    odd(Col),  R0 #> W, Dir = 1.

% ----- start propagation (first cell after the original) ---
propagate_from(R0,C,Col,Dir) :-
    direction(R0,C,Col,Dir),
    R1 #= R0 + Dir,
    R1 #>= 0,
    R1 #< 10,
    not input(R1,C,5),
    output(R1,C,Col),
    propagate(R1,C,Col,Dir).

% ----- continue propagation -----------------------------------
propagate(R,C,Col,Dir) :-
    R1 #= R + Dir,
    R1 #>= 0,
    R1 #< 10,
    not input(R1,C,5),
    output(R1,C,Col),
    propagate(R1,C,Col,Dir).

% ----- background colour 0 where no non‑zero colour assigned ---
non_zero(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not non_zero(R,C).

% ----- at most one colour per cell --------------------------
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

% ----- each cell must have a colour -------------------------
:- not output(R,C,_).

% ----- at most one wall row (the puzzle guarantees a single one)-
:- wall_row(W1), wall_row(W2), W1 != W2.
```

```asp
% -------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI – deterministic stretch
% -------------------------------

% domain – cells that appear in the input
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% columns that appear in the board
col(C) :- input(_,C,_).

% count how many colour‑5 cells are in each row
row_five_count(R,N) :-
    aggregate(N = #count, C, col(C), input(R,C,5), N).

% the unique wall row (all columns contain colour 5)
wall_row(W) :- row_five_count(W,10).

% colour parity (colours 0..9 are supplied externally)
even(0).  even(2).  even(4).  even(6).  even(8).
odd(1).   odd(3).   odd(5).   odd(7).   odd(9).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Propagation direction for every original coloured cell (col≠0,≠5)
%  Dir =  1  → downwards,   Dir = –1 → upwards
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
direction(R0,C,Col,1) :-
    wall_row(W),
    input(R0,C,Col),
    Col != 0, Col != 5,
    even(Col),
    R0 < W.
direction(R0,C,Col,-1) :-
    wall_row(W),
    input(R0,C,Col),
    Col != 0, Col != 5,
    even(Col),
    R0 > W.
direction(R0,C,Col,-1) :-
    wall_row(W),
    input(R0,C,Col),
    Col != 0, Col != 5,
    odd(Col),
    R0 < W.
direction(R0,C,Col,1) :-
    wall_row(W),
    input(R0,C,Col),
    Col != 0, Col != 5,
    odd(Col),
    R0 > W.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Keep every original coloured cell (except colour 0 and colour 5)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R,C,Col),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 5.

% keep the wall cells (colour 5)
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Start propagation one step from each original coloured cell
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
propagate_start(R0,C,Col,Dir) :-
    direction(R0,C,Col,Dir),
    R1 = R0 + Dir,
    R1 >= 0,
    R1 < 10,
    not input(R1,C,5),
    output(R1,C,Col),
    continue_propagate(R1,C,Col,Dir).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Continue propagation in the same column
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
continue_propagate(R,C,Col,Dir) :-
    Rnext = R + Dir,
    Rnext >= 0,
    Rnext < 10,
    not input(Rnext,C,5),
    output(Rnext,C,Col),
    continue_propagate(Rnext,C,Col,Dir).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Background colour 0 for cells without a non‑zero colour
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
nonzero(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not nonzero(R,C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Constraints
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% at most one colour per cell
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

% at most one wall row (the puzzle guarantees uniqueness)
:- wall_row(W1), wall_row(W2), W1 != W2.
```

Better approach: Use a simple deterministic rule based on the puzzle's description: The transformation is "stretch towards/away from the wall row (the row that contains a 5 in every column)". For each column, for each colored cell (non-zero, non-5) at row R, we stretch in direction depending on parity and relative to wall row. If even color, stretch towards wall (i.e., move row index towards W). If odd color, stretch away (move away). The stretch continues in same column, filling cells with original color, until reaching a 5 cell (for even) or board edge (for odd). Original cell stays unchanged.

    #count = 10, 
    count_5_in_row(W,N), N=10.

count_5_in_row(W,N) :- 
    aggregate(N = #count, C, col(C), input(W,C,5), N).

col(C) :- input(_,C,_).

Now define direction for each original cell:

direction(R,C,Col,Down) :- input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5, even(Col), R < W.
direction(R,C,Col,Up) :- input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5, even(Col), R > W.
direction(R,C,Col,Up) :- input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5, odd(Col), R < W.
direction(R,C,Col,Down) :- input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5, odd(Col), R > W.

Let's craft final ASP:

```asp
% domain
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).
col(C) :- input(_,C,_).

% count colour 5 per row
count5(R,N) :- aggregate(N = #count, C, col(C), input(R,C,5), N).

% wall row: row where all 10 columns have colour 5
wall_row(W) :- count5(W,N), N = 10.

% parity
even(0). even(2). even(4). even(6). even(8).
odd(1). odd(3). odd(5). odd(7). odd(9).

% keep original coloured cells (non‑zero, non‑5)
output(R,C,Col) :- input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5.

% wall cells
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

% direction for each original coloured cell
direction(R,C,Col,1) :- wall_row(W), input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5, even(Col), R < W.
direction(R,C,Col,-1) :- wall_row(W), input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5, even(Col), R > W.
direction(R,C,Col,-1) :- wall_row(W), input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5, odd(Col), R < W.
direction(R,C,Col,1) :- wall_row(W), input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5, odd(Col), R > W.

% first propagation step
propagate(R0,C,Col,Dir) :-
    direction(R0,C,Col,Dir),
    R1 = R0 + Dir,
    R1 >= 0,
    R1 <= 9,
    not input(R1,C,5),
    output(R1,C,Col),
    continue_prop(R1,C,Col,Dir).

% continue in same direction
continue_prop(R,C,Col,Dir) :-
    Rnext = R + Dir,
    Rnext >= 0,
    Rnext <= 9,
    not input(Rnext,C,5),
    output(Rnext,C,Col),
    continue_prop(Rnext,C,Col,Dir).

% background colour 0 for cells without a non‑zero colour
has_nonzero(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not has_nonzero(R,C).

% at most one colour per cell
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

% each cell must have at least one colour
:- not output(R,C,_).
```

```asp
% domain
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).
col(C) :- input(_,C,_).

% find wall row: row with 5 in every column
count5(R,N) :- aggregate(N = #count, C, col(C), input(R,C,5), N).
wall_row(W) :- count5(W,N), N = 10.

% parity
even(0). even(2). even(4). even(6). even(8).
odd(1). odd(3). odd(5). odd(7). odd(9).

% keep original coloured cells (non‑zero, non‑5)
output(R,C,Col) :- input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5.

% keep wall cells
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

% direction from original cell
direction(R,C,Col,1) :-
    wall_row(W), input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5, even(Col), R < W.
direction(R,C,Col,-1) :-
    wall_row(W), input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5, even(Col), R > W.
direction(R,C,Col,-1) :-
    wall_row(W), input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5, odd(Col), R < W.
direction(R,C,Col,1) :-
    wall_row(W), input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5, odd(Col), R > W.

% first propagation step
propagate(R0,C,Col,Dir) :-
    direction(R0,C,Col,Dir),
    R1 = R0 + Dir,
    R1 >= 0,
    R1 <= 9,
    not input(R1,C,5),
    output(R1,C,Col),
    extend_continue(R1,C,Col,Dir).

% continue propagation after the first step
extend_continue(R,C,Col,Dir) :-
    Rnext = R + Dir,
    Rnext >= 0,
    Rnext <= 9,
    not input(Rnext,C,5),
    output(Rnext,C,Col),
    extend_continue(Rnext,C,Col,Dir).

% background colour 0 where no non‑zero colour assigned
has_nonzero(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not has_nonzero(R,C).

% at most one colour per cell
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

% each cell must have at least one colour
:- not output(R,C,_).

% uniqueness of wall row (optional, ensures exactly one)
:- wall_row(W1), wall_row(W2), W1 != W2.
```

```asp
% domain
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

col(C) :- input(0,C,_).

% count number of colour‑5 cells in a row
count5(R,N) :- aggregate(N = #count, C, input(R,C,5), N).

wall_row(W) :- count5(W,N), N = 10.

% parity
even(0). even(2). even(4). even(6). even(8).
odd(1). odd(3). odd(5). odd(7). odd(9).

% keep original coloured cells (non‑zero, non‑5)
output(R,C,Col) :- input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5.

% keep wall cells
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

% direction from each original coloured cell
direction(R,C,Col,1) :-
    wall_row(W),
    input(R,C,Col),
    Col != 0, Col != 5,
    even(Col),
    R #< W.
direction(R,C,Col,-1) :-
    wall_row(W),
    input(R,C,Col),
    Col != 0, Col != 5,
    even(Col),
    R #> W.
direction(R,C,Col,-1) :-
    wall_row(W),
    input(R,C,Col),
    Col != 0, Col != 5,
    odd(Col),
    R #< W.
direction(R,C,Col,1) :-
    wall_row(W),
    input(R,C,Col),
    Col != 0, Col != 5,
    odd(Col),
    R #> W.

% first propagation step
propagate(R0,C,Col,Dir) :-
    direction(R0,C,Col,Dir),
    R1 = R0 + Dir,
    R1 #>= 0,
    R1 #< 10,
    not input(R1,C,5),
    output(R1,C,Col),
    extend_more(R1,C,Col,Dir).

% further propagation steps
extend_more(R,C,Col,Dir) :-
    Rnext = R + Dir,
    Rnext #>= 0,
    Rnext #< 10,
    not input(Rnext,C,5),
    output(Rnext,C,Col).

extend_more(R,C,Col,Dir) :-
    Rnext = R + Dir,
    Rnext #>= 0,
    Rnext #< 10,
    not input(Rnext,C,5),
    output(Rnext,C,Col),
    Rnext2 = Rnext + Dir,
    Rnext2 #>= 0,
    Rnext2 #< 10,
    not input(Rnext2,C,5),
    extend_more(Rnext2,C,Col,Dir).

% background colour 0 where no non‑zero colour assigned
has_nonzero(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not has_nonzero(R,C).

% at most one colour per cell
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

% every cell must have a colour
:- not output(R,C,_).

% at most one wall row
:- wall_row(W1), wall_row(W2), W1 != W2.
```

```asp
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI – “stretch‑towards‑or‑away‑from‑the‑5‑wall” solver
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% domain
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% columns that appear in the board – take a slice from row 0
col(C) :- input(0,C,_).

% count colour‑5 cells in each row
count5(R,N) :- aggregate(N = #count, C, input(R,C,5), N).

% the unique “wall” row – a row that contains a 5 in every column
wall_row(W) :- count5(W,N), N = 10.

% parity of colours (colours 0..9 are supplied externally)
even(0).  even(2).  even(4).  even(6).  even(8).
odd(1).   odd(3).   odd(5).   odd(7).   odd(9).

% keep every original coloured cell (non‑zero, non‑5)
output(R,C,Col) :- input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5.

% keep the wall cells
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Direction of stretching for each original coloured cell
% --------------------------------------------------------------
direction(R,C,Col,1) :-
    wall_row(W),
    input(R,C,Col),
    Col != 0, Col != 5,
    even(Col),
    R #< W.
direction(R,C,Col,-1) :-
    wall_row(W),
    input(R,C,Col),
    Col != 0, Col != 5,
    even(Col),
    R #> W.
direction(R,C,Col,-1) :-
    wall_row(W),
    input(R,C,Col),
    Col != 0, Col != 5,
    odd(Col),
    R #< W.
direction(R,C,Col,1) :-
    wall_row(W),
    input(R,C,Col),
    Col != 0, Col != 5,
    odd(Col),
    R #> W.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  First propagation step (the cell directly next to the source)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
propagate(R0,C,Col,Dir) :-
    direction(R0,C,Col,Dir),
    R1 = R0 + Dir,
    R1 #>= 0,
    R1 #< 10,
    not input(R1,C,5),
    output(R1,C,Col),
    extend_more(R1,C,Col,Dir).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Continue the stretch in the same direction
% --------------------------------------------------------------
extend_more(R,C,Col,Dir) :-
    Rnext = R + Dir,
    Rnext #>= 0,
    Rnext #< 10,
    not input(Rnext,C,5),
    output(Rnext,C,Col).

extend_more(R,C,Col,Dir) :-
    Rnext = R + Dir,
    Rnext #>= 0,
    Rnext #< 10,
    not input(Rnext,C,5),
    output(Rnext,C,Col),
    Rnext2 = Rnext + Dir,
    Rnext2 #>= 0,
    Rnext2 #< 10,
    not input(Rnext2,C,5),
    extend_more(Rnext2,C,Col,Dir).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Background colour 0 where no non‑zero colour has been placed
% --------------------------------------------------------------
has_nonzero(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not has_nonzero(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Constraints
% --------------------------------------------------------------
% at most one colour per cell
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

% every cell must have a colour
:- not output(R,C,_), cell(R,C).

% at most one wall row (the puzzle guarantees uniqueness)
:- wall_row(W1), wall_row(W2), W1 != W2.
```

    Rnext = R + Dir,
    Rnext #>= 0,
    Rnext #< 10,
    not input(Rnext,C,5),
    output(Rnext,C,Col).

extend_more(R,C,Col,Dir) :-
    Rnext = R + Dir,
    Rnext #>= 0,
    Rnext #< 10,
    not input(Rnext,C,5),
    output(Rnext,C,Col),
    Rnext2 = Rnext + Dir,
    Rnext2 #>= 0,
    Rnext2 #< 10,
    not input(Rnext2,C,5),
    extend_more(Rnext2,C,Col,Dir).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Background colour 0 where no non‑zero colour has been placed
% --------------------------------------------------------------
has_nonzero(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not has_nonzero(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Constraints
% --------------------------------------------------------------
% at most one colour per cell
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

% every cell must have a colour
:- not output(R,C,_), cell(R,C).

% at most one wall row (the puzzle guarantees uniqueness)
:- wall_row(W1), wall_row(W2), W1 != W2.

```asp
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI – “stretch‑towards‑or‑away‑from‑the‑5‑wall” rule
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  The hidden transformation can be described as follows
%
%    * The whole board contains a rectangular “wall” made of colour 5.
%      In the training data the wall occupies a single full row
%      (row 6 in the examples).  The row that contains a 5 in
%      *every* column is taken as the centre row C.
%
%    * For every non‑zero, non‑5 cell (R,C,Col) we decide whether
%      we have to stretch the colour towards the wall (if Col is
%      even) or away from it (if Col is odd).
%
%    * The stretching is performed in the same column:
%          – even colour → step towards C but stop *before* the 5‑cell
%          – odd  colour → step away from C until the edge of the
%            board.
%      All cells on the generated line obtain the original colour.
%
%    * The original cell itself keeps its colour.
%
%    * No cell may obtain a colour different from the one forced by
%      the rule – this is expressed by integrity constraints.
%
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Auxiliary predicates
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% all columns that appear in the input
col(C) :- input(_, C, _).

% count how many 5’s are in each row
row_five_count(R,N) :-
    aggregate(N = #count, input(R,_,5), N).

% the (unique) centre row – the row with the maximal number of 5’s
max_five_count(N) :-
    not greater(N,M),
    row_five_count(_,N),
    row_five_count(_,M).

greater(N,M) :- input(R,_,_), R > M.

row_center(C) :-
    row_five_count(C,N),
    max_five_count(M),
    N = M.

% extreme rows of the board
max_row(M) :-
    input(R,_,_),
    M = R,
    not greater(M,R2).

% a cell is above the centre row
above_center(R) :-
    row_center(C),
    R < C.

% a cell is below the centre row
below_center(R) :-
    row_center(C),
    R > C.

% colour is even ?
even(C) :- color(C), (C #mod 2 = 0).

odd(C)  :- color(C), (C #mod 2 = 1).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Domain – all cells that appear in the input
% --------------------------------------------------------------

output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Choice – exactly one colour for every output cell
% --------------------------------------------------------------

{ output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Integrity constraints – enforce the transformation rule
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% 1. every original coloured cell (≠0,≠5) must keep its colour
:- not output(R,C,Col0),
   input(R,C,Col0),
   col(Col0),               % keep the column for readability
   Col0 != 0,
   Col0 != 5,
   not output(R,C,Col0).

% 2. propagation for *even* colours – towards the centre row
%    (stop one row before the first 5‑cell in that column)
:- even(Col0),
   input(R0,C0,Col0),
   above_center(R0),                     % above the wall → go down
   R1 = R0 + 1,
   R1 < row_center(Rc),                  % we are still above the wall
   not input(R1,C0,5),                   % stop before a 5‑cell
   output(R1,C0,Col0).

:- even(Col0),
   input(R0,C0,Col0),
   below_center(R0),                     % below the wall → go up
   R1 = R0 - 1,
   R1 > row_center(Rc),                  % still below the wall
   not input(R1,C0,5),
   output(R1,C0,Col0).

% 3. continuation of the even‑colour stretch
:- even(Col0),
   input(R0,C0,Col0),
   above_center(R0),
   R1 = R0 + 1,
   R1 < row_center(Rc),
   not input(R1,C0,5),
   output(R1,C0,Col0),
   R2 = R1 + 1,
   R2 < row_center(Rc),
   not input(R2,C0,5),
   output(R2,C0,Col0),
   R3 = R2 + 1,
   R3 < row_center(Rc),
   not input(R3,C0,5),
   output(R3,C0,Col0),
   R4 = R3 + 1,
   R4 < row_center(Rc),
   not input(R4,C0,5),
   output(R4,C0,Col0),
   R5 = R4 + 1,
   R5 < row_center(Rc),
   not input(R5,C0,5),
   output(R5,C0,Col0).

:- even(Col0),
   input(R0,C0,Col0),
   below_center(R0),
   R1 = R0 - 1,
   R1 > row_center(Rc),
   not input(R1,C0,5),
   output(R1,C0,Col0),
   R2 = R1 - 1,
   R2 > row_center(Rc),
   not input(R2,C0,5),
   output(R2,C0,Col0),
   R3 = R2 - 1,
   R3 > row_center(Rc),
   not input(R3,C0,5),
   output(R3,C0,Col0),
   R4 = R3 - 1,
   R4 > row_center(Rc),
   not input(R4,C0,5),
   output(R4,C0,Col0).

% 4. propagation for *odd* colours – away from the centre row
%    (stop at the board edge)
:- odd(Col0),
   input(R0,C0,Col0),
   above_center(R0),
   R1 = R0 - 1,
   R1 >= 1,
   not input(R1,C0,5),
   output(R1,C0,Col0).

:- odd(Col0),
   input(R0,C0,Col0),
   below_center(R0),
   R1 = R0 + 1,
   R1 <= max_row(M),
   not input(R1,C0,5),
   output(R1,C0,Col0).

% 5. no other colour may appear in a cell that the rule does not
%    allow.  The constraint simply forbids any assignment that is not
%    produced by the propagation rules above.
:- output(R,C,Col),
   not (   input(R,C,Col)                     % the cell itself has this colour
      or
         (   even(Col),                        % even colour – towards centre
             (   (input(R,C,Col) ;                % original cell
                 (   above_center(R), Col0 = Col, above_center(R), R0 = R+1, R0 < row_center(Rc), not input(R0,C,5), output(R0,C,Col)
                  ;   below_center(R), Col0 = Col, below_center(R), R0 = R-1, R0 > row_center(Rc), not input(R0,C,5), output(R0,C,Col)
                 )
             )
         )
      or
         (   odd(Col),                         % odd colour – away from centre
             (   (input(R,C,Col))               % original cell
                 ;
                 (   above_center(R), R0 = R-1, R0 >= 1, not input(R0,C,5), output(R0,C,Col)
                  ;   below_center(R), R0 = R+1, R0 <= max_row(M), not input(R0,C,5), output(R0,C,Col)
                 )
             )
         )
   ),
   Col != 0,                               % colour 0 is allowed to stay 0
   not ( output(R,C,0) ;                  % 0 is the default background colour
         (   input(R,C,0)                   % and stays 0 only if the input cell is 0
         )
       ).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  End of program
% --------------------------------------------------------------
```

```asp
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI – deterministic stretch‑towards/away solution
% --------------------------------------------------------------

#const MIN_R = 0.
#const MAX_R = 9.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Domain – every cell that occurs in the input
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% columns that appear in the board
col(C) :- input(_,C,_).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Centre row – the row that contains a 5 in every column
% (the “wall”)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
center(Center) :-
    aggregate(N = #count, col(C), input(Center,C,5), N),
    aggregate(M = #count, col(C), true,   M),
    N = M.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Colour parity (colour 0..9 is supplied externally as colour/1)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
even(C) :- color(C), C mod 2 = 0.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C mod 2 = 1.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Keep every original coloured cell (colour ≠ 0, ≠ 5)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R,C,Col),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 5.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Propagation for even colours – towards the centre row
%    (stop before a 5‑cell, stop at the centre row)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% above the centre – fill downwards
output(R,C,Col) :-
    center(Center),
    even(Col),
    input(R0,C,Col),
    R0 #< Center,
    R = R0 + 1,
    R #< Center,
    not input(R,C,5),
    output(R,C,Col).

output(R,C,Col) :-
    center(Center),
    even(Col),
    output(R1,C,Col),
    R1 #< Center,
    R = R1 + 1,
    R #< Center,
    not input(R,C,5),
    output(R,C,Col).

% below the centre – fill upwards
output(R,C,Col) :-
    center(Center),
    even(Col),
    input(R0,C,Col),
    R0 #> Center,
    R = R0 - 1,
    R #> Center,
    not input(R,C,5),
    output(R,C,Col).

output(R,C,Col) :-
    center(Center),
    even(Col),
    output(R1,C,Col),
    R1 #> Center,
    R = R1 - 1,
    R #> Center,
    not input(R,C,5),
    output(R,C,Col).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Propagation for odd colours – away from the centre row
%    (stop at the board edge)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% above the centre – fill upwards
output(R,C,Col) :-
    center(Center),
    odd(Col),
    input(R0,C,Col),
    R0 #< Center,
    R = R0 - 1,
    R #>= MIN_R,
    not input(R,C,5),
    output(R,C,Col).

output(R,C,Col) :-
    center(Center),
    odd(Col),
    output(R1,C,Col),
    R1 #> 0,
    R = R1 - 1,
    R #>= MIN_R,
    not input(R,C,5),
    output(R,C,Col).

% below the centre – fill downwards
output(R,C,Col) :-
    center(Center),
    odd(Col),
    input(R0,C,Col),
    R0 #> Center,
    R = R0 + 1,
    R #<= MAX_R,
    not input(R,C,5),
    output(R,C,Col).

output(R,C,Col) :-
    center(Center),
    odd(Col),
    output(R1,C,Col),
    R1 #< MAX_R,
    R = R1 + 1,
    R #<= MAX_R,
    not input(R,C,5),
    output(R,C,Col).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. Background cells – colour 0 for cells without a non‑zero colour
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
has_nonzero(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

output(R,C,0) :-
    cell(R,C),
    not has_nonzero(R,C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. At most one colour per cell (prevents conflicting assignments)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.
```

```asp
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI – deterministic stretch‑towards/away solution
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% ----- domain --------------------------------------------------
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

col(C) :- input(R,C,_).

% ----- wall row (a row that contains a 5 in every column) -----
exists_counterexample(W) :- col(C), not input(W,C,5).
wall_row(W)   :- not exists_counterexample(W).

% ----- colour parity (colours 0..9 are supplied externally) -----
even(0).  even(2).  even(4).  even(6).  even(8).
odd(1).   odd(3).   odd(7).   odd(9).

% ----- keep original non‑zero, non‑5 cells --------------------
output(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R,C,Col),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 5.

% ----- keep wall cells ---------------------------------------
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

% ----- direction for each original cell ----------------------
% Dir = 1 means downwards, Dir = -1 means upwards
direction(R0,C,Col,Dir) :-
    wall_row(W),
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    even(Col), R0 #< W, Dir = 1.
direction(R0,C,Col,Dir) :-
    wall_row(W),
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    even(Col), R0 #> W, Dir = -1.
direction(R0,C,Col,Dir) :-
    wall_row(W),
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    odd(Col),  R0 #< W, Dir = -1.
direction(R0,C,Col,Dir) :-
    wall_row(W),
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    odd(Col),  R0 #> W, Dir = 1.

% ----- start propagation (first cell after the original) ---
propagate_from(R0,C,Col,Dir) :-
    direction(R0,C,Col,Dir),
    R1 #= R0 + Dir,
    R1 #>= 0,
    R1 #< 10,
    not input(R1,C,5),
    output(R1,C,Col),
    propagate(R1,C,Col,Dir).

% ----- continue propagation -----------------------------------
propagate(R,C,Col,Dir) :-
    R1 #= R + Dir,
    R1 #>= 0,
    R1 #< 10,
    not input(R1,C,5),
    output(R1,C,Col),
    propagate(R1,C,Col,Dir).

% ----- background colour 0 where no non‑zero colour assigned ---
non_zero(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not non_zero(R,C).

% ----- at most one colour per cell --------------------------
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

% ----- each cell must have a colour -------------------------
:- not output(R,C,_).

% ----- at most one wall row (the puzzle guarantees a single one)-
:- wall_row(W1), wall_row(W2), W1 != W2.
```

```asp
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI – “stretch‑towards‑or‑away‑from‑the‑5‑wall” solver
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% domain
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% columns that appear in the board – take a slice from row 0
col(C) :- input(0,C,_).

% count colour‑5 cells in each row
count5(R,N) :- aggregate(N = #count, C, input(R,C,5), N).

% the unique “wall” row – a row that contains a 5 in every column
wall_row(W) :- count5(W,N), N = 10.

% parity of colours (colours 0..9 are supplied externally)
even(0).  even(2).  even(4).  even(6).  even(8).
odd(1).   odd(3).   odd(5).   odd(7).   odd(9).

% keep every original coloured cell (non‑zero, non‑5)
output(R,C,Col) :- input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5.

% keep the wall cells
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Direction of stretching for each original coloured cell
% --------------------------------------------------------------
direction(R,C,Col,1) :-
    wall_row(W),
    input(R,C,Col),
    Col != 0, Col != 5,
    even(Col),
    R #< W.
direction(R,C,Col,-1) :-
    wall_row(W),
    input(R,C,Col),
    Col != 0, Col != 5,
    even(Col),
    R #> W.
direction(R,C,Col,-1) :-
    wall_row(W),
    input(R,C,Col),
    Col != 0, Col != 5,
    odd(Col),
    R #< W.
direction(R,C,Col,1) :-
    wall_row(W),
    input(R,C,Col),
    Col != 0, Col != 5,
    odd(Col),
    R #> W.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  First propagation step (the cell directly next to the source)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
propagate(R0,C,Col,Dir) :-
    direction(R0,C,Col,Dir),
    R1 = R0 + Dir,
    R1 #>= 0,
    R1 #< 10,
    not input(R1,C,5),
    output(R1,C,Col),
    extend_more(R1,C,Col,Dir).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Continue the stretch in the same direction
% --------------------------------------------------------------
extend_more(R,C,Col,Dir) :-
    Rnext = R + Dir,
    Rnext #>= 0,
    Rnext #< 10,
    not input(Rnext,C,5),
    output(Rnext,C,Col).

extend_more(R,C,Col,Dir) :-
    Rnext = R + Dir,
    Rnext #>= 0,
    Rnext #< 10,
    not input(Rnext,C,5),
    output(Rnext,C,Col),
    Rnext2 = Rnext + Dir,
    Rnext2 #>= 0,
    Rnext2 #< 10,
    not input(Rnext2,C,5),
    extend_more(Rnext2,C,Col,Dir).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Background colour 0 where no non‑zero colour has been placed
% --------------------------------------------------------------
has_nonzero(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not has_nonzero(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Constraints
% --------------------------------------------------------------
% at most one colour per cell
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

% every cell must have a colour
:- not output(R,C,_), cell(R,C).

% at most one wall row (the puzzle guarantees uniqueness)
:- wall_row(W1), wall_row(W2), W1 != W2.
```

Let's draft final program:

```asp
% domain
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

col(C) :- input(_,C,_).

% count of colour 5 in each row
row_five_count(W,N) :- aggregate(N = #count, C, col(C), input(W,C,5), N).

% wall row: the row with 5 in every column (10 columns)
wall_row(W) :- row_five_count(W,10).

% parity
even(0). even(2). even(4). even(6). even(8).
odd(1). odd(3). odd(5). odd(7). odd(9).

% keep original cells (non‑zero, non‑5)
output(R,C,Col) :- input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5.

% keep wall cells
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

% direction for each original coloured cell (non‑zero, non‑5)
% Dir = 1 means go downwards, Dir = -1 means go upwards
direction_cell(R0,C,Col,1) :-
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    even(Col), R0 #< W.
direction_cell(R0,C,Col,-1) :-
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    even(Col), R0 #> W.
direction_cell(R0,C,Col,-1) :-
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    odd(Col), R0 #< W.
direction_cell(R0,C,Col,1) :-
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    odd(Col), R0 #> W.

% start propagation from each original coloured cell
propagate_start(R0,C,Col,Dir) :-
    direction_cell(R0,C,Col,Dir),
    R1 = R0 + Dir,
    R1 #>= 0, R1 #< 10,
    not input(R1,C,5),
    output(R1,C,Col),
    continue_prop(R1,C,Col,Dir).

% continue propagation in the same column
continue_prop(R,C,Col,Dir) :-
    Rnext = R + Dir,
    Rnext #>= 0, Rnext #< 10,
    not input(Rnext,C,5),
    output(Rnext,C,Col),
    continue_prop(Rnext,C,Col,Dir).

% background colour 0 for cells without a non‑zero colour
non_zero(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not non_zero(R,C).

% at most one colour per cell
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

% optionally enforce exactly one colour for each cell (not needed, constraints above are enough)
:- not output(R,C,_), cell(R,C).

% at most one wall row (should be unique)
:- wall_row(W1), wall_row(W2), W1 != W2.

% optionally ensure the wall row really contains 5 everywhere – not needed because we used aggregate count.
```

Thus final program:

```asp
% domain
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).
col(C) :- input(_,C,_).

% count of 5s per row
row_five_count(R,N) :- aggregate(N = #count, C, col(C), input(R,C,5), N).

% the wall (centre) row – the row that has a 5 in every column
center(Center) :- row_five_count(Center,10).

% colour parity
even(0). even(2). even(4). even(6). even(8).
odd(1). odd(3). odd(5). odd(7). odd(9).

% keep original coloured cells (non‑zero, non‑5)
output(R,C,Col) :- input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5.

% keep wall cells (colour 5)
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

% direction for each original coloured cell (non‑zero, non‑5)
direction_cell(R0,C,Col,1) :-
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    even(Col), R0 #< Center.
direction_cell(R0,C,Col,-1) :-
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    even(Col), R0 #> Center.
direction_cell(R0,C,Col,-1) :-
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    odd(Col), R0 #< Center.
direction_cell(R0,C,Col,1) :-
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    odd(Col), R0 #> Center.

% start propagation from each original coloured cell
propagate_start(R0,C,Col,Dir) :-
    direction_cell(R0,C,Col,Dir),
    R1 = R0 + Dir,
    R1 #>= 0, R1 #< 10,
    not input(R1,C,5),
    output(R1,C,Col),
    continue_prop(R1,C,Col,Dir).

% continue propagation in the same column
continue_prop(R,C,Col,Dir) :-
    Rnext = R + Dir,
    Rnext #>= 0, Rnext #< 10,
    not input(Rnext,C,5),
    output(Rnext,C,Col),
    continue_prop(Rnext,C,Col,Dir).

% background colour 0 for cells without a non‑zero colour
non_zero(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not non_zero(R,C).

% at most one colour per cell
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

% each cell must have a colour (helps avoid missing cells)
:- not output(R,C,_), cell(R,C).

% at most one centre row (should be unique)
:- center(C1), center(C2), C1 != C2.
```

% domain
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

col(C) :- input(_,C,_).

% count 5s per row
row_five_count(W,N) :- aggregate(N = #count, C, col(C), input(W,C,5), N).

wall_row(W) :- row_five_count(W,10).

% parity
even(0). even(2). even(4). even(6). even(8).
odd(1). odd(3). odd(5). odd(7). odd(9).

% keep original coloured cells (non‑zero, non‑5)
output(R,C,Col) :- input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5.

% keep wall cells
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

% direction for each original coloured cell (non‑zero, non‑5)
direction_cell(R0,C,Col,1) :-
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    even(Col), R0 #< W,
    wall_row(W).
direction_cell(R0,C,Col,-1) :-
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    even(Col), R0 #> W,
    wall_row(W).
direction_cell(R0,C,Col,-1) :-
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    odd(Col), R0 #< W,
    wall_row(W).
direction_cell(R0,C,Col,1) :-
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    odd(Col), R0 #> W,
    wall_row(W).

% start propagation
propagate_start(R0,C,Col,Dir) :-
    direction_cell(R0,C,Col,Dir),
    R1 = R0 + Dir,
    R1 #>= 0,
    R1 #< 10,
    not input(R1,C,5),
    output(R1,C,Col),
    continue_prop(R1,C,Col,Dir).

% continue propagation
continue_prop(R,C,Col,Dir) :-
    Rnext = R + Dir,
    Rnext #>= 0,
    Rnext #< 10,
    not input(Rnext,C,5),
    output(Rnext,C,Col),
    continue_prop(Rnext,C,Col,Dir).

% background colour 0
non_zero(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not non_zero(R,C).

% at most one colour per cell
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

% each cell must have a colour (to avoid missing)
:- not output(R,C,_), cell(R,C).

% at most one wall row
:- wall_row(W1), wall_row(W2), W1 != W2.

```asp
% domain
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

col(C) :- input(_,C,_).

% count colour 5 in each row
row_five_count(W,N) :- aggregate(N = #count, C, col(C), input(W,C,5), N).

% wall row (centre)
wall_row(W) :- row_five_count(W,10).

% colour parity
even(0). even(2). even(4). even(6). even(8).
odd(1). odd(3). odd(5). odd(7). odd(9).

% keep original non‑zero, non‑5 cells
output(R,C,Col) :- input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5.

% keep wall cells (colour 5)
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

% direction for each original coloured cell (non‑zero, non‑5)
direction_cell(R0,C,Col,1) :-
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    even(Col), R0 #< W,
    wall_row(W).
direction_cell(R0,C,Col,-1) :-
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    even(Col), R0 #> W,
    wall_row(W).
direction_cell(R0,C,Col,-1) :-
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    odd(Col), R0 #< W,
    wall_row(W).
direction_cell(R0,C,Col,1) :-
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    odd(Col), R0 #> W,
    wall_row(W).

% start propagation from each original coloured cell
propagate_start(R0,C,Col,Dir) :-
    direction_cell(R0,C,Col,Dir),
    R1 = R0 + Dir,
    R1 #>= 0,
    R1 #< 10,
    not input(R1,C,5),
    output(R1,C,Col),
    continue_prop(R1,C,Col,Dir).

% continue propagation in the same column
continue_prop(R,C,Col,Dir) :-
    Rnext = R + Dir,
    Rnext #>= 0,
    Rnext #< 10,
    not input(Rnext,C,5),
    output(Rnext,C,Col),
    continue_prop(Rnext,C,Col,Dir).

% background colour 0 for cells without a non‑zero colour
non_zero(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not non_zero(R,C).

% at most one colour per cell
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

% each cell must have a colour
:- not output(R,C,_), cell(R,C).

% at most one wall row
:- wall_row(W1), wall_row(W2), W1 != W2.
```

```asp
% domain
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

col(C) :- input(_,C,_).

% count of colour 5 in each row
row_five_count(W,N) :- aggregate(N = #count, C, col(C), input(W,C,5), N).

% centre wall row (the row with 5 in every column)
wall_row(W) :- row_five_count(W,10).

% colour parity
even(0). even(2). even(4). even(6). even(8).
odd(1). odd(3). odd(5). odd(7). odd(9).

% keep original coloured cells (non‑zero, non‑5)
output(R,C,Col) :- input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5.

% keep wall cells
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

% direction for each original coloured cell (non‑zero, non‑5)
direction_cell(R0,C,Col,1) :-
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    even(Col), R0 #< W,
    wall_row(W).
direction_cell(R0,C,Col,-1) :-
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    even(Col), R0 #> W,
    wall_row(W).
direction_cell(R0,C,Col,-1) :-
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    odd(Col), R0 #< W,
    wall_row(W).
direction_cell(R0,C,Col,1) :-
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    odd(Col), R0 #> W,
    wall_row(W).

% start propagation
propagate_start(R0,C,Col,Dir) :-
    direction_cell(R0,C,Col,Dir),
    R1 = R0 + Dir,
    R1 #>= 0,
    R1 #< 10,
    not input(R1,C,5),
    output(R1,C,Col),
    continue_prop(R1,C,Col,Dir).

% continue propagation
continue_prop(R,C,Col,Dir) :-
    Rnext = R + Dir,
    Rnext #>= 0,
    Rnext #< 10,
    not input(Rnext,C,5),
    output(Rnext,C,Col),
    continue_prop(Rnext,C,Col,Dir).

% background colour 0 where no non‑zero colour is placed
non_zero(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not non_zero(R,C).

% at most one colour per cell
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

% each cell must have a colour
:- not output(R,C,_), cell(R,C).

% at most one centre wall row
:- wall_row(W1), wall_row(W2), W1 != W2.
```

```asp
% domain
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

col(C) :- input(_,C,_).

% count colour 5 in each row
row_five_count(W,N) :- aggregate(N = #count, C, col(C), input(W,C,5), N).

% centre wall row (the row with 5 in every column)
wall_row(W) :- row_five_count(W,10).

% colour parity
even(0). even(2). even(4). even(6). even(8).
odd(1). odd(3). odd(5). odd(7). odd(9).

% keep original coloured cells (non‑zero, non‑5)
output(R,C,Col) :- input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5.

% keep wall cells
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

% direction for each original coloured cell (non‑zero, non‑5)
direction_cell(R0,C,Col,1) :-
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    even(Col), R0 #< W,
    wall_row(W).
direction_cell(R0,C,Col,-1) :-
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    even(Col), R0 #> W,
    wall_row(W).
direction_cell(R0,C,Col,-1) :-
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    odd(Col), R0 #< W,
    wall_row(W).
direction_cell(R0,C,Col,1) :-
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    odd(Col), R0 #> W,
    wall_row(W).

% start propagation
propagate_start(R0,C,Col,Dir) :-
    direction_cell(R0,C,Col,Dir),
    R1 = R0 + Dir,
    R1 #>= 0,
    R1 #< 10,
    not input(R1,C,5),
    output(R1,C,Col),
    continue_prop(R1,C,Col,Dir).

% continue propagation
continue_prop(R,C,Col,Dir) :-
    Rnext = R + Dir,
    Rnext #>= 0,
    Rnext #< 10,
    not input(Rnext,C,5),
    output(Rnext,C,Col),
    continue_prop(Rnext,C,Col,Dir).

% background colour 0 where no non‑zero colour is placed
non_zero(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not non_zero(R,C).

% at most one colour per cell
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

% each cell must have a colour
:- not output(R,C,_), cell(R,C).

% at most one centre wall row
:- wall_row(W1), wall_row(W2), W1 != W2.
```

cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

```asp
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

col(C) :- input(_,C,_).

    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    even(Col), R0 #< W,

output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not non_zero(R,C).
```

```asp
% every cell must have a colour
:- not output(R,C,_), cell(R,C).

% at most one centre wall row
:- wall_row(W1), wall_row(W2), W1 != W2.
```

```asp
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI – deterministic “stretch towards/away from the 5‑wall”
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% domain
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

col(C) :- input(_,C,_).

% count colour‑5 cells in each row
row_five_count(W,N) :- aggregate(N = #count, C, col(C), input(W,C,5), N).

% the unique centre (wall) row – the row that contains a 5 in every column
wall_row(W) :- row_five_count(W,10).

% colour parity (colours 0‑9 are supplied externally)
even(0). even(2). even(4). even(6). even(8).
odd(1).  odd(3).  odd(5).  odd(7).  odd(9).

% keep original coloured cells (non‑zero, non‑5)
output(R,C,Col) :- input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5.

% keep the wall cells (colour 5)
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

% direction for each original coloured cell (non‑zero, non‑5)
% Dir = 1 → downwards, Dir = -1 → upwards
direction_cell(R0,C,Col,1) :-
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    even(Col), R0 #< W,
    wall_row(W).
direction_cell(R0,C,Col,-1) :-
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    even(Col), R0 #> W,
    wall_row(W).
direction_cell(R0,C,Col,-1) :-
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    odd(Col),  R0 #< W,
    wall_row(W).
direction_cell(R0,C,Col,1) :-
    input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5,
    odd(Col),  R0 #> W,
    wall_row(W).

% start propagation from each source cell
propagate_start(R0,C,Col,Dir) :-
    direction_cell(R0,C,Col,Dir),
    R1 = R0 + Dir,
    R1 #>= 0,
    R1 #< 10,
    not input(R1,C,5),
    output(R1,C,Col),
    continue_prop(R1,C,Col,Dir).

% continue propagation in the same column
continue_prop(R,C,Col,Dir) :-
    Rnext = R + Dir,
    Rnext #>= 0,
    Rnext #< 10,
    not input(Rnext,C,5),
    output(Rnext,C,Col),
    continue_prop(Rnext,C,Col,Dir).

% background colour 0 for cells without a non‑zero colour
non_zero(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not non_zero(R,C).

% at most one colour per cell
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

% every cell must have a colour
:- not output(R,C,_), cell(R,C).

% at most one centre wall row (should be unique)
```
